codeReview.summary: Implementation is mostly complete but has one blocking issue: `McpToolWrapper` doesn't implement `behavioral_hints()` from the `Tool` trait, so MCP tools always return the default `ToolBehavioralHints` instead of reflecting the actual MCP annotations (`readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`). This means the MCP tool annotations are parsed and stored but not actually exposed via the `Tool` trait interface.

issues.description: `McpToolWrapper` implements the `Tool` trait but does NOT implement `behavioral_hints()`. All other built-in tools in swell-tools (ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ShellTool, GitTool, FileEditTool, SearchTool, WebSearchTool) implement `behavioral_hints()` and return their specific hints. The MCP tool wrapper should map its `McpToolAnnotations` to `ToolBehavioralHints` so that callers using the `Tool` trait interface get the correct behavioral hints. Currently, `McpToolWrapper::behavioral_hints()` returns the trait default (`read_only_hint: false, destructive_hint: false, idempotent_hint: true`) instead of reflecting the actual MCP annotations.

`McpToolAnnotations::is_read_only()` returns `self.read_only_hint.unwrap_or(false)` - when the hint is `None`, the tool is treated as non-read-only. This is a reasonable default but could be documented. Similarly, `is_destructive()` defaults to `false` and `is_idempotent()` defaults to `true`.

summary: The `McpToolAnnotations` struct (with `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`) is correctly parsed from MCP server responses and stored in `McpToolInfo`. The `risk_level_from_annotations()` and `permission_tier()` methods on `McpToolInfo` and `McpToolWrapper` correctly map annotations to `ToolRiskLevel` and `PermissionTier`. However, `McpToolWrapper` does not implement the `behavioral_hints()` method from the `Tool` trait, so the MCP tool annotations are not exposed to callers using the standard `Tool` trait interface. This is a blocking issue because callers expecting behavioral hints via the trait will get incorrect defaults.
